editorial.pullQuote: A district court in Suwon, Gyeonggi Province, issued an arrest warrant on Sunday for a 51-year-old drug dealer.
summary: A Suwon district court issued an arrest warrant for a 51-year-old drug dealer repatriated to Korea in May.
The case highlights cross-border drug trafficking networks and enforcement challenges.
Authorities are intensifying efforts against international narcotics operations.
editorial.stance: Editorial calls for stronger international cooperation to combat transnational drug operations.

editorials.body: 60대 이상이 빚투 1위, 고령사회 또 다른 뇌관 우려 올 들어 4월까지 60대 이상 투자자가 빚을 내 주식에 투자한 신용 거래 융자 잔고의 증가율이 33%를 넘기며 전 연령대에서 1위에 올랐다. 금액도 약 2조600억원으로 늘어, 50대2조400억원와 40대1
editorials.pullQuote: 60대 이상이 빚을 내 주식에 투자한 신용 거래 융자 잔고의 증가율이 33%를 넘기며 전 연령대에서 1위에 올랐다.
summary: 60대 이상 투자자의 신용 거래 융자 잔고가 33% 증가해 전 연령대 1위.
빚내 투자한 고령층 규모가 2조600억원으로 급증하는 추세.
고령사회의 위험한 투자 관행이 새로운 사회적 뇌관으로 우려된다.

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Another Fetal Death From Labor Room Delays—How Much Longer Can We Tolerate This?
summaryTr: Another fetal death amid hospital transfers for delivery—how much longer must we tolerate this? A pregnant woman in Cheongju, North Chungcheong, unable to find a hospital for emergency delivery, was transferred all the way to Busan, where her fetus died.
In late February, a pregnant woman in Daegu underwent surgery in Bundang, Gyeonggi, four hours after calling 119, but one of her twins died.

editorial.body: 입력 종합 26면 더불어민주당 주도로 파죽지세로 발의된 조작기소 의혹 특검 법안이 갑자기 속도 조절 국면을 맞았다. 어제 이재명 대통령이 “구체적인 시기나 절차는 민주당이 국민적 의견 수렴과 숙의 과정을 거쳐 판단해 달라”는 메시지를 내면서다. 민주당은 “당내 여러 의견을 논의하겠다”고 호응하는 모습이다. 이 대통령과 민주당이 특검 법안 처리 속도를 늦추는 것은 6·3 지방선거 때문이다. 특검 법안의 위헌성 등 본질적 문제점에 귀를 닫고 있다가 영남권 등 선거 판세에 미치는 악영향에 대한 우려가 커지자 뒤늦게 대응하는 것이다. 민주당은 특검 법안이 ‘최고 권력자의 공소 취소를 위해 법치주의의 근간을 훼손한다’는 반대 여론에 꿈쩍도 하지 않았다. 위법적 국정조사를 추진하고 사실관계가 확정되지 않았는데도 조작기소로 예단하고 “두고 볼 수 없다”고 목소리를 높였다. 어제도 이 대통령과 민주당은 “특검을 통해 진실을 규명하고 사법적 정의를 바로 세우는 것은 반드시 해야 할 일”이라고 했다. 그러는 사이 대통령 지지율이 50%대로 떨어지고 지방선거 격전지의 지지율 격차가 줄어드는 여론조사 결과가 나오자 반대 여론을 잠재울 필요성을 느끼게 된 것이다. 일단 선거에 이긴 뒤 특검 법안을 통과시키고 이 대통령의 공소 취소를 실현하겠다는 계산이다. 국민을 두 번 속이려는 의도로밖에 보이지 않는 상황이다. 이준석 개혁신당 대표는 “국민을 기만하려는 시간 끌기”라고 꼬집었다. 야권의 수도권 광역단체장 후보들은 이 대통령을 향해 “공소 취소는 절대 없다고 선언하라”고 요구했다. 반대 여론의 핵심은 ‘대통령의, 대통령에 의한, 대통령을 위한 특검’이라는 점으로 요약된다. 이 대통령이 연루된 대부분의 사건에서 공소 취소를 포함한 면죄부를 마련하고 가해자를 응징하겠다는 게 특검 법안의 골자이기 때문이다. 재심 등 기존 법 체계에서 해결할 수 있는 일을 이 대통령이 임명한 특검을 통해 원스톱으로 해결하는 전형적인 ‘위인설법’인 것이다. 영장전담법관을 법원이 별도로 지정해 사법부 독립 침해의 소지도 있다. 법치주의 훼손의 수혜자는 이 대통령 한 명뿐이다. 선거 이후로 입법을 연기하려는 것도 이를 인정하기 때문 아닌가.
editorial.pullQuote: 일단 선거에 이긴 뒤 특검 법안을 통과시키고 이 대통령의 공소 취소를 실현하겠다는 계산이다.
summary: 민주당, 선거 영향 우려해 특검 법안 속도 조절로 전환했다.
위헌성 지적 외면하다 지지율 하락하자 뒤늦게 대응하는 형국이다.
선거 승리 후 통과 목표로 국민을 두 번 속이려는 의도로 보인다.
editorial.stance: 민주당의 특검 법안은 대통령 1인만을 위한 위인설법으로, 법치주의 훼손의 악법이다.
editorial.titleTr: [사설] 선거용 특검 법안 연기, 국민 두 번 속이는 격이다
summaryTr: The Democratic Party has shifted to slowing down the special prosecutor bill due to concerns about electoral impact.
After ignoring constitutional concerns, it is now belatedly responding as its approval ratings have declined.
It appears the party intends to deceive the public twice by aiming to pass the bill after winning the election.
editorial.bodyTr: The special prosecution bill on alleged manipulated indictments, which the Democratic Party pushed through at breakneck speed, has suddenly hit the brakes. This came after President Lee Jae-myung said yesterday that "the Democratic Party should make a judgment after gathering public opinion and deliberation on the specific timing and procedures." The Democratic Party responded by saying it would "discuss various internal opinions." President Lee and the Democratic Party are slowing down the processing of the special prosecution bill because of the June 3 local elections. They had closed their ears to fundamental issues like the bill's unconstitutionality, but as concerns grew about its negative impact on the electoral landscape in the Yeongnam region and elsewhere, they belatedly took action. The Democratic Party remained unmoved by public opposition that the special prosecution bill "undermines the foundation of the rule of law to overturn the prosecution of the highest authority." They pushed forward with unlawful state investigations and prejudged the matter as manipulated indictment despite unconfirmed facts, raising their voices saying "this cannot stand." Even yesterday, President Lee and the Democratic Party said "clarifying the truth and establishing judicial justice through special prosecution is something that must be done." But as approval ratings for the president fell to the 50 percent range and poll results showed narrowing support gaps in key election battlegrounds, they felt the need to suppress opposing voices. Their calculation is to win the election first, then pass the special prosecution bill and realize the president's prosecution overturn. This appears to be nothing but an attempt to deceive the public twice. Reform Party leader Lee Jun-seok pointed out that this was "stalling to deceive the people." Opposition candidates for metropolitan government positions demanded that President Lee "declare that a prosecution overturn will absolutely not happen." The core of the opposing public opinion can be summed up as "special prosecution of the president, by the president, for the president." The special prosecution bill essentially provides absolution including prosecution overturn in most cases involving President Lee and aims to punish perpetrators. This is a typical example of "rule by the virtuous"—resolving through a special prosecutor appointed by President Lee what could be handled through existing legal systems including retrial. There is also potential concern about judicial independence violation, as warrant-dedicated judges would be separately designated by the courts. The sole beneficiary of undermining the rule of law is President Lee alone. Isn't their attempt to postpone legislation after the election an admission of this?

editorial.title: No one should judge own case
editorial.body: Democratic Party bill could let indictments against Lee be withdrawn before courts rule President Lee Jae Myung had been facing five criminal trials before they were suspended following his presidential election victory. The Daejang-dong development scandal, Ssangbangwool’s alleged illegal transfer of US dollars to North Korea for then-Gyeonggi Gov. Lee’s visit and his alleged misappropriation of the Gyeonggi provincial budget were stopped at the first-trial stage; the subornation of perjury case at the appellate stage; and the election law violation case at the remand stage after the Supreme Court’s decision against him. These trials are expected to resume after his presidential term ends in June 2030. However, a so-called special counsel bill on alleged indictment manipulation, proposed by the ruling Democratic Party of Korea on April 30, would allow a special counsel to decide whether to maintain the prosecutions in all of those cases. In effect, it would grant the special counsel the authority to withdraw the indictments. The party proposed the bill, citing a recent National Assembly investigation that they say confirmed alleged fabrication of indictments. Yet cases involving President Lee, which were not even addressed in that probe, were included within the scope of the proposed special counsel investigation. If a special counsel recommended by the ruling party-dominated National Assembly were to withdraw indictments despite prosecutors' opposition — and thereby nullify court adjudication — it would raise serious concerns. The majority party’s political power is infringing not only on prosecutorial authority but also on judicial authority itself. Moreover, the special counsel would be appointed by the president. This means that a defendant would appoint the special counsel to investigate his own case and possibly withdraw the indictments against him, effectively erasing the case. This runs directly counter to the legal maxim "Nemo iudex in causa sua" ("no one should be a judge in his own cause") and represents a serious conflict of interest by any standard. The Democratic Party says a special counsel is needed, claiming that prosecutorial fabrication of indictments was uncovered during a National Assembly probe. However, the parliamentary inquiry revealed virtually no evidence of fabrication, such as coercion of false testimony or document falsification. At a National Assembly hearing, former Ssangbangwool chairman Kim Seong-tae denied allegations that prosecutors had hosted a "salmon and drinks party" in a storage room at their office for him and another defendant to induce favorable testimony. The Democratic Party claimed it was false that Kim had handed over US dollars in the Philippines to Ri Ho-nam, a North Korean, to cover the cost of then-Gyeonggi Gov. Lee’s visit to the North. However, that claim lost credibility following testimony at the National Assembly by Bang Yong-cheol, the company’s former vice chairman. The National Assembly investigation was reportedly led by Democratic Party lawmaker Lee Geon-tae, who previously served as Lee’s attorney in the Daejang-dong case. He formed a group of lawmakers to push for the withdrawal of indictments in Lee’s cases and to pursue a National Assembly investigation. In the end, the Assembly probe became the groundwork for proposing the bill and seeking the withdrawal of indictments against Lee. If Lee is innocent, the truth can be established later in court. With his trials on hold during his term, they will not interfere with his conduct of state affairs. If, as the ruling party claims, the indictments were indeed fabricated, the court will acquit him. Some defendants may have legitimate grievances regarding the charges against them, but any injustice should be remedied through the courts. If this principle does not apply to someone, it is nothing more than a special privilege. The bill grants such privilege to only one person. If the bill passes as is, it will undermine the very foundations of democracy: The rule of law, the separation of powers and basic common sense and principles. The opposition must unite in voicing its opposition to the Democratic Party’s unilateral push, backed by its absolute majority. editorials.title: No one should judge own case
editorials.body: Democratic Party bill could let indictments against Lee be withdrawn before courts rule President Lee Jae Myung had been facing five criminal trials before they were suspended following his presidential election victory. The Daejang-dong development scandal, Ssangbangwool’s alleged illegal transfer of US dollars to North Korea for then-Gyeonggi Gov. Lee’s visit and his alleged misappropriation of the Gyeonggi provincial budget were stopped at the first-trial stage; the subornation of perjury case at the appellate stage; and the election law violation case at the remand stage after the Supreme Court’s decision against him. These trials are expected to resume after his presidential term ends in June 2030. However, a so-called special counsel bill on alleged indictment manipulation, proposed by the ruling Democratic Party of Korea on April 30, would allow a special counsel to decide whether to maintain the prosecutions in all of those cases. In effect, it would grant the special counsel the authority to withdraw the indictments. The party proposed the bill, citing a recent National Assembly investigation that they say confirmed alleged fabrication of indictments. Yet cases involving President Lee, which were not even addressed in that probe, were included within the scope of the proposed special counsel investigation. If a special counsel recommended by the ruling party-dominated National Assembly were to withdraw indictments despite prosecutors' opposition — and thereby nullify court adjudication — it would raise serious concerns. The majority party’s political power is infringing not only on prosecutorial authority but also on judicial authority itself. Moreover, the special counsel would be appointed by the president. This means that a defendant would appoint the special counsel to investigate his own case and possibly withdraw the indictments against him, effectively erasing the case. This runs directly counter to the legal maxim "Nemo iudex in causa sua" ("no one should be a judge in his own cause") and represents a serious conflict of interest by any standard. The Democratic Party says a special counsel is needed, claiming that prosecutorial fabrication of indictments was uncovered during a National Assembly probe. However, the parliamentary inquiry revealed virtually no evidence of fabrication, such as coercion of false testimony or document falsification. At a National Assembly hearing, former Ssangbangwool chairman Kim Seong-tae denied allegations that prosecutors had hosted a "salmon and drinks party" in a storage room at their office for him and another defendant to induce favorable testimony. The Democratic Party claimed it was false that Kim had handed over US dollars in the Philippines to Ri Ho-nam, a North Korean, to cover the cost of then-Gyeonggi Gov. Lee’s visit to the North. However, that claim lost credibility following testimony at the National Assembly by Bang Yong-cheol, the company’s former vice chairman. The National Assembly investigation was reportedly led by Democratic Party lawmaker Lee Geon-tae, who previously served as Lee’s attorney in the Daejang-dong case. He formed a group of lawmakers to push for the withdrawal of indictments in Lee’s cases and to pursue a National Assembly investigation. In the end, the Assembly probe became the groundwork for proposing the bill and seeking the withdrawal of indictments against Lee. If Lee is innocent, the truth can be established later in court. With his trials on hold during his term, they will not interfere with his conduct of state affairs. If, as the ruling party claims, the indictments were indeed fabricated, the court will acquit him. Some defendants may have legitimate grievances regarding the charges against them, but any injustice should be remedied through the courts. If this principle does not apply to someone, it is nothing more than a special privilege. The bill grants such privilege to only one person. If the bill passes as is, it will undermine the very foundations of democracy: The rule of law, the separation of powers and basic common sense and principles. The opposition must unite in voicing its opposition to the Democratic Party’s unilateral push, backed by its absolute majority. editorials.pullQuote: 개헌안 처리를 위한 국회 본회의가 오는 7일 열린다.
summary: 국회 본회의가 7일 개헌안 투표를 진행한다.
여야 6개 정당이 개헌안을 공동 발의했으나 국민의힘은 제외됐다.
국힘은 당론 반대를 풀고 의원들 소신 투표를 허용해야 한다.
editorials.stance: 국민의힘은 개헌 반대 당론을 풀어 의원들의 자유로운 소신 투표를 보장해야 한다.
editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Power Future Party Should Release Anti-Constitutional Reform Party Line and Trust Members' Judgment
summaryTr: The National Assembly will hold a vote on the constitutional amendment bill on the 7th.
Six ruling and opposition parties jointly proposed the constitutional amendment bill, but the People Power Party was excluded.
The People Power Party should withdraw its party line opposition and allow its members to vote according to their consciences.
editorials.bodyTr: A National Assembly plenary session to handle the constitutional amendment will be held on the 7th. Depending on the voting results that day, it will also be decided whether a constitutional amendment referendum will be held simultaneously with the June 3 local elections. Previously, six ruling and opposition parties excluding the People Power Party—including the Democratic Party of Korea, the Innovation Party for the Nation, the Progressive Party, and the Reform Party—jointly proposed a constitutional amendment bill on the 3rd of last month.

Citigroup lowered Samsung Electronics' target price from 320,000 won to 300,000 won..

summaryTr: The semiconductor boom is masking polarization across the broader industry.
Growth centered on some large conglomerates is deepening the gap with small and medium-sized enterprises.
Balanced development across all industries is urgently needed.
